title: Xenophyllum digitatum
taxonomy: Xenophyllum digitatum belongs to the family Plantaginaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family includes several genera and species, and Xenophyllum digitatum is one of them. The plant is further classified into the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Within the order Lamiales, Xenophyllum digitatum is classified as a species within the genus Xenophyllum. The genus Xenophyllum itself is part of the family Plantaginaceae and is native to the Americas.

intro: Xenophyllum digitatum, also known as "Aguevedo," is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential treatments for gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Xenophyllum digitatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
